Network activity shows no IPs, domains, or HTTP conversations, which is consistent with the IOC summary. This suggests the malware might be offline or the network activity wasn't captured in this sand

Registry changes show no persistence keys or other keys, which is surprising given the file artifacts in Adobe and Flash Player directories. These files might be used for social engineering or as part

The dropped files tool returns 0 files, which is inconsistent with the initial overview that listed 30 files written. This discrepancy suggests the tools might be aggregating data differently or the i

The process activity shows no processes created, no mutexes created, and only two notable modules (msctf.dll and comctl32.dll). This is very sparse data compared to the initial overview which listed 3
